name: psocfw
what_this_is: F2 -- the SoC boot FIRMWARE (the guest half of 'boot + console output + clean exit', the milestone sw/ps/ven_soc_app documents). Unlike the pboot test stubs, this is a real firmware: it initialises the COM1 16550 UART and prints a banner to it, then exits. On the board the PS app's service_out routes the COM1 THR bytes (0x3F8) to the Linux console.
transitions: reset (F000:FFF0) -> 16-bit real mode; DS=0xF000 so the banner (in the -bios segment) is addressable; cr0 stays 0x60000010
print the banner 'Ventium SoC: Pentium (P54C) core alive on KV260\r\n' char-by-char via LODSB (DS:SI) -> OUT COM1 THR (0x3F8)
why_per_record_differential: Every step is synchronous + deterministic (the PS-emulated / RTL-modeled COM1 THR always accepts a byte, so the bytes are written back-to-back with no LSR poll -> no I/O timing nondeterminism). The gen_trace.py --system single-step golden is a valid per-record oracle; compare.py --mode func checks the boot + every UART OUT per-record over all 324 retired instructions. The banner text ('Ventium SoC: Pentium (P54C) core alive on KV260') is reproduced byte-identical on qemu-system's serial.
non_vacuous: The gate asserts BOTH traces reach the exit (pc=0x3c) AFTER the full print loop -- a HALT or a short banner would never get there.
zero_new_rtl_note: No new RTL: runs on the existing ventium_soc 16550 UART + the LODSB string engine + flat real-mode memory. The banner read uses the string engine (DS:SI) because register-indirect [si] is not decoded (only [disp16] + the string engine are); the F000-segment DS makes the in-image banner reachable.
next: F3 adds INT-based BIOS services (INT 10h/13h/16h/21h), which need real-mode IVT INT n / IRET delivery (currently only the protected-mode 8-byte IDT gate path exists) -- the next firmware-foundation RTL feature.
description: Bare-metal F2 SoC boot firmware. A real-mode -bios firmware inits COM1 and prints a banner, then exits; proves the SoC boots + does console output byte-identical to qemu-system-i386 -cpu pentium over all 324 retired instructions, per-record, with a non-vacuous banner-printed-then-exit assertion. The guest half of the F2 'boot + console + clean exit' milestone.
